title: Final Music
wikitext:
[]: Final Music             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sun Jul 14, 2013 1:46 pm With the excellent and thought provoking music we've had up to the end of 3.0/Q I decided to go through my Refrain soundtrack again, and I thought that Everything You've Ever Dreamed would be a good choice for the ending music or song selection, possibly Instrumentality or other climatic moment for 4.0/Final. Any thoughts? P.S.: I did Ctr+F and use the search engine to see if there was thread like this anywhere on the site and it didn't come up, but if there is I apologize in advance and politlely ask a mod to move it to the appropiate place.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Jul 14, 2013 2:18 pm I just want a remix of Komm Susser Tod.

[]: Re: Final Music             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Jul 14, 2013 3:22 pm Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: Everything You've Ever Dreamed

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Jul 14, 2013 3:54 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I just want a remix of Komm Susser Tod.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jul 14, 2013 4:19 pm Q had a new song to end. I imagine Final will as well, though I suppose it could just about reprise Sakura Nagashi , depending on how the story goes.

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Sun Jul 14, 2013 4:31 pm Re-arrange of previous tracks are enough for me. They've gone with new composition for vocal tracks so far and they probably will again in the final film. But if I had to choose a piece for the re-arrangement (that which wasn't actually used, like OP mentions), I'd say Eternal Embrace.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Jul 14, 2013 4:34 pm I'm hoping for a new Utada Hikaru song. BUT I could see them doing a remix of Sakura Nagashi

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jul 14, 2013 9:24 pm I hope for some rendition of Heisoku no Kakudai, and a good old fashioned Borderline Case mix to end it.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun Jul 14, 2013 9:58 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I just want a remix of Komm Susser Tod.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Jul 14, 2013 10:27 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: uh we can of already have that she also did a dubstep version

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Sun Jul 14, 2013 10:49 pm Even though she's officially on hiatus, she probably will do another song if asked since she is a fan as well. Let's hope that they do, so we can get some new lovely tunes out of her.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun Jul 14, 2013 10:51 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: Oh God, that sounds absolutely awful. Never mind, just give me another Hikaru song. I just want more ear sex from her lovely, lovely voice.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon Jul 15, 2013 9:20 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: I'm hoping for a new Utada Hikaru song. BUT I could see them doing a remix of Sakura Nagashi

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Jul 17, 2013 9:56 pm The more I listen to them, the more the Rebuild soundtracks remind me of video game music, especially with the need for epic choir in every 3.0 song. They also seem too... melodic? When you listen to a given track from any of the movies, it doesn't flow with the action in a natural way. The song follows its course like they wrote it and later decided it would go well with a given scene, instead of scoring the film after the fact. It's not bad music, but it makes me feel like I'm watching a video game cutscene instead of a high budget theatrical film. I really don't want to pick on 2.0, but it has the biggest issue with this; the giant monster attacks are given final fantasy music. There's no musical cues or any association with the onscreen action. They have this big 15 minute action sequence against one monster, but instead of giving the battle a coherent musical identity (bs term I don't know how to describe it) they wrote 4 completely different songs that were 'epic' and had them playlisted in the background as the boss changed forms. What I want in 4.0, but am guaranteed not to get, is an actual film score.

[]:             Quote Post by Newser » Wed Jul 24, 2013 11:41 am I'd be happy to hear either Beautiful World remix or Sakura Nagashi remix as ending song (which is unlikely though)

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Wed Jul 24, 2013 12:15 pm J.A. Seazer should do it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Feb 26, 2014 2:16 am Three words: Komm Susser Tod.

[]: Re: Final Music             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Feb 26, 2014 3:55 am Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: Everything You've Ever Dreamed

[]:             Quote Post by artesox » Wed Feb 26, 2014 9:59 am Final has to end with fly me to the moon, otherwise I will be displeased

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Wed Feb 26, 2014 10:26 am Ending theme has to be Sakura Nagashi or something new.
summary: []: Final Music             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sun Jul 14, 2013 1:46 pm With the excellent and thought provoking music we've had up to the end of 3.0/Q I decided to go through my Refrain soundtrack again, and I thought that Everything You've Ever Dreamed would be a good choice for the ending music or song selection, possibly Instrumentality or other climatic moment for 4.0/Final. Any thoughts? P.S.: I did Ctr+F
